 976 ## [28/30] Indirect Procurement Q1 2026 Newsletter
 977 Source: gmail | Thread: 19d3df93c2c73f8f | Labels: Inbox, Forums | Priority: None | Senders: 'Indirect Procureme. | Last Date: Mon, Mar 30, 2026, 9:00 AM | Last Updated: 2026-03-30T10:01:47.698304+00:00
 978 **Executive Briefing: Indirect Procurement Q1 2026 Newsletter**
 979 
 980 **Key Participants & Roles**
 981 *   **Sender:** Project Buywell Team (via `grp_fpg_all@fairpricegroup.sg` and `project_buywell@fairpricegroup.sg`).
 982 *   **Audience:** All FairPrice Group colleagues ("Colleagues").
 983 *   **Stakeholders:** Indirect Procurement representatives, Finance team, Suppliers, and Subject Matter Experts (SMEs).
 984 
 985 **Main Topic**
 986 The newsletter outlines the final preparation phase for the "Project Buywell" indirect procurement system transition scheduled for Go-Live in Q1 2026. It introduces key features including the new **eInvoice module**, automated **Delegation of Authority (DOA)** streams, and clarifies usage boundaries between **Zycus eProc** and **MyClaims**.
 987 
 988 **Pending Actions & Ownership**
 989 *   **System Testing:** Users must prepare for User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and upcoming Key-User Testing/End-User Training sessions. *Owner: All Employees.*
 990 *   **Training Compliance:** Employees must review feature spotlights to prepare for a mandatory quiz prior to Go-Live. Winners will receive prizes. *Owner: All Employees.*
 991 *   **Process Adherence:** Users must stop using MyClaims for non-personal expenses and route such purchases to the correct dedicated systems (e.g., Zycus eProc for assets). *Owner: All Employees.*
 992 *   **System Confirmation:** Recipients are requested to click a specific link (`https://forms.gle/D6whAz364PKpPznx5`) to confirm they have read the newsletter. *Owner: All Employees.*
 993 
 994 **Decisions Made**
 995 1.  **eInvoice Implementation:** Adoption of a digital-first eInvoice module to replace paper processing, enabling automated 3-way matching (PO, GR, Invoice) and real-time status tracking.
 996 2.  **DOA Automation:** Approval streams will be automatically routed based on spend type:
 997     *   Standard Spend.
 998     *   Waiver Approval (Waiver of Competition/Tender).
 999     *   SME Approval (mandatory for technical exceptions like IT/Cybersecurity).
1000     *   Mobile approvals enabled for approvers.
1001 3.  **Expense Policy Enforcement:** Strict prohibition on using credit card charge slips; only original itemized receipts are accepted. MyClaims is restricted strictly to personal expenses incurred while working.
1002 
1003 **Key Dates & Deadlines**
1004 *   **Newsletter Date:** March 30, 2026.
1005 *   **Go-Live:** Imminent (Q1 2026 closure). A quiz will be administered immediately before Go-Live.
1006 *   **Follow-up:** Training session emails are forthcoming; no specific dates provided yet.
1007 
1008 **Specific References**
1009 *   **Systems Mentioned:** Project Buywell, Zycus eProc, MyClaims, eInvoice module.
1010 *   **Contact Email:** `project_buywell@fairpricegroup.sg`.
1011 *   **Quiz Link:** Refer to "Ready for a Quiz?" section (link provided in original email).
1012 *   **Sustainability Impact:** Digital invoicing cited as saving ~118 trees per 1M invoices and reducing carbon footprint by 63%.
1013 
1014

1049 ## [30/30] GCP Service Account Key clean UP
1050 Source: gmail | Thread: 195892088be2d83b | Labels: Inbox | Priority: None | Senders: Mohit, Kyle, Himal | Last Date: Mon, Mar 30, 2026, 7:36 AM | Last Updated: 2026-03-30T10:02:27.546231+00:00
1051 **Daily Work Briefing: GCP Service Account Key Cleanup (Updated)**
1052 
1053 **1. Key Participants & Roles**
1054 *   **Himal Hewagamage** (himal.hewagamage@fairpricegroup.sg): Initiator and Security Lead; followed up on March 30, 2026, regarding non-production remediation status.
1055 *   **Kyle Nguyen**: Technical lead coordinating immediate cleanup; assigned Nicholas Tan to assist starting the week of March 30, 2026.
1056 *   **Nicholas Tan**: New support resource added by Kyle Nguyen to assist with execution and tracking.
1057 *   **Jazz Tong** (jazz_tong@fairpricegroup.sg): Previously identified as execution lead; role context remains relevant for scope verification.
1058 *   **Mohit Niranwal** (mohit_niranwal@fairpricegroup.sg): Provided initial context on automated rotation gaps and legacy accounts.
1059 
1060 **2. Main Topic/Request**
1061 The team is addressing security risks associated with legacy GCP Service Account (SA) keys created between 2019–2024. Following the March 24, 2026, directive to remediate 56 keys in non-production environments, a status check on **March 30, 2026**, revealed no prior updates from Kyle's team. Consequently, work is now formally restarting with Nicholas Tan as support. Additionally, Himal confirmed the removal of old Datadog service account keys and updated the tracking sheet accordingly.
1062 
1063 **3. Pending Actions & Owners**
1064 *   **Immediate Remediation (Non-Prod)**: Kyle Nguyen and Nicholas Tan will commence execution for the 56 identified legacy keys starting the week of March 30, 2026.
1065 *   **Progress Tracking**: Kyle Nguyen has added a "Status" column to the tracking sheet to monitor individual item progress; this is now the primary method for status reporting.
1066 *   **Documentation Update**: Kyle must update the [GCP][Security] Service Account Key Rotation & Decommission document to reflect project owner information and current remediation status.
1067 *   **Owner Verification**: Integration of Jazz Tong's previous task (tagging application owners) remains pending under Kyle's documentation responsibility.
1068 *   **Onboarding Process**: For SAs without keys or those with outdated keys, the team will facilitate onboarding into the automated key rotation process via a specific Google Group.
1069 
1070 **4. Decisions Made & Process Changes**
1071 *   **Execution Strategy (Mar 30, 2026)**: The team confirmed that remediation for non-production environments will proceed immediately with expanded resources (Nicholas Tan).
1072 *   **Data Hygiene**: Himal Hewagamage has proactively removed old Datadog service account keys and updated the central tracking sheet.
1073 *   **Communication Channel**: A separate weekly communication channel remains the primary method for status updates regarding key decommissioning.
1074 *   **Scope & Strategy**: The scope covers `cpd-dp`, `fpg-callisto`, `fpg-jarvis`, `fpg-optimus`, and `fponprd` environments. The strategy prioritizes enabling automated rotation for legacy accounts before decommissioning based on application utility.
1075 
1076 **5. Key Dates & Follow-ups**
1077 *   **Mar 10–24, 2026**: Initial requests, spreadsheet updates, meeting, and directive for immediate remediation of 56 keys issued by Himal.
1078 *   **Mar 30, 2026 (4:11 AM)**: Himal requested a status update on non-production key removal, noting no response was received.
1079 *   **Mar 30, 2026 (7:33 AM)**: Kyle confirmed the team will start work that week and added Nicholas Tan to support; tracking sheet updated with a new "Status" column.
1080 *   **Mar 30, 2026 (7:36 AM)**: Himal confirmed removal of old Datadog keys and offered further support if clarification is needed.
1081 
1082 **Links & References**
1083 *   *Data Source*: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mGBCTRQDcTs0z_w0LjVtywDCtFHZhZfWYIRGqcNglpQ/edit?gid=0#gid=0 (Updated by Himal)
1084 *   *Documentation*: [GCP][Security] Service Account Key Rotation & Decommission (To be updated by Kyle).
